R Markdown Code: Assessing the Impact of the 21st Century Assistive Technology Act on Technology Access for Disabled Americans
Contents: Libaries, Datasets, Descriptive Statistics, Models
Loading Libraries
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
## ✔ ggplot2   3.5.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.0.4     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(flextable)
## 
## Attaching package: 'flextable'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     compose
library(fixest)
library(papaja)
## Loading required package: tinylabels
## 
## Attaching package: 'papaja'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:fixest':
## 
##     se
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:flextable':
## 
##     theme_apa
library(psych)
## 
## Attaching package: 'psych'
## 
## The following objects are masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     %+%, alpha
library(modelsummary)
## 
## Attaching package: 'modelsummary'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:psych':
## 
##     SD
library("rdrobust")
Loading Datasets
df1 <- read.csv("/Users/chelseaxie/Downloads/stategrantsyears.csv")
df2 <- read.csv("/Users/chelseaxie/Downloads/REAL FIXED EFFECTS DATA - fixed effect loans.csv")
df3 <- read_csv("/Users/chelseaxie/Downloads/combined_internet_descriptive - combined_internet_descriptive.csv")
## Rows: 4 Columns: 3
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## dbl (3): year, disabled, not
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
df4 <- read_csv("/Users/chelseaxie/Downloads/combined_device_descriptive - combined_device_descriptive.csv")
## Rows: 4 Columns: 3
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## dbl (3): year, prdisflg_1, prdisflg_2
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
df5 <- read_csv("/Users/chelseaxie/Downloads/grants data + descriptive stats - GRANT DATA ALL YEARS.csv")
## Rows: 51 Columns: 10
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (1): state
## dbl (9): y2016, y2017, y2018, y2019, y2020, y2021, y2022, y2023, y2024
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
df6 <- read_csv("/Users/chelseaxie/Downloads/Merged_Loans_and_Grants_Data - Merged_Loans_and_Grants_Data.csv")
## Rows: 459 Columns: 5
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (1): state
## dbl (3): year, grant, policy
## num (1): loans
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
df7<-read_csv("/Users/chelseaxie/Downloads/fixedeffectscomplete.csv")
## Rows: 204 Columns: 7
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr (1): state
## dbl (5): year, grant, internet, device, policy
## num (1): loans
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
Descriptive Statistics
Grants & Loans Dumbbell Plots
Grants
df_long <- pivot_longer(df1, 
                        cols = starts_with("y"), 
                        names_to = "year", 
                        names_prefix = "y", 
                        values_to = "loans") %>%
  mutate(year = as.numeric(year)) %>%
  filter(year != 2020)

df_avg <- df_long %>%
  mutate(period = if_else(year < 2022, "Pre-2022", "Post-2022")) %>%
  group_by(state, period) %>%
  summarise(avg_loans = mean(loans, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(period = factor(period, levels = c("Pre-2022", "Post-2022")))


df_wide <- df_avg %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = period, values_from = avg_loans)
# Dumbbell-style chart with log scale
ggplot(df_wide, aes(y = reorder(state, `Post-2022`))) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = `Pre-2022`, xend = `Post-2022`, yend = state), color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(x = `Pre-2022`), color = "salmon", size = 3) +
  geom_point(aes(x = `Post-2022`), color = "skyblue", size = 3) +
  scale_x_log10() +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9, margin = margin(r = 5), color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 17)
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Change in Average Grant Allocation: Pre-2022 vs Post-2022",
    x = "Average Grant Allocation (log scale)", y = "State"
  )


Loans
colnames(df2)[3] <- "loans"
df2$loans <- as.numeric(gsub(",", "", df2$loans))

df2 <- df2 %>%
  mutate(period = if_else(policy == 0, "Pre-2022", "Post-2022"))

df_avg <- df2 %>%
  group_by(state, period) %>%
  summarise(avg_loans = mean(loans, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = period, values_from = avg_loans)
ggplot(df_avg, aes(y = reorder(state, `Post-2022`))) +
  geom_segment(aes(x = `Pre-2022`, xend = `Post-2022`, yend = state), color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(x = `Pre-2022`, color = "Pre-2022"), size = 3) +
  geom_point(aes(x = `Post-2022`, color = "Post-2022"), size = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(
    name = "Period",
    values = c("Pre-2022" = "salmon", "Post-2022" = "skyblue")
  ) +
  scale_x_sqrt() +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9, margin = margin(r = 5), color = "black"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10, color = "black"),
    plot.title = element_text(size = 15, color = "black"),
    axis.title.x = element_text(color = "black"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.title = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.text = element_text(color = "black")
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Change in Average Loans: Pre-2022 vs Post-2022",
    x = "Average Number of Loans (sqrt scale)", y = "State"
  )


Internet and Digital Device Plots
Internet Access
ggplot(df3, aes(x = year)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = disabled, color = "Disabled"), size = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(y = disabled, color = "Disabled"), size = 2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = not, color = "Not Disabled"), size = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(y = not, color = "Not Disabled"), size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(df3$year), max(df3$year), by = 2)) +  # only odd years will show
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 0.9)) +
  labs(
    title = "Internet Access by Disability Status",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Proportion with Internet Access",
    color = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title= element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.x= element_text(size = 15),
    axis.title.y= element_text(size = 15)
  )
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.


Digital Device Access
ggplot(df4, aes(x = year)) +
  geom_line(aes(y = prdisflg_1, color = "Disabled"), size = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(y = prdisflg_1, color = "Disabled"), size = 2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = prdisflg_2, color = "Not Disabled"), size = 1) +
  geom_point(aes(y = prdisflg_2, color = "Not Disabled"), size = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(min(df4$year), max(df4$year), by = 2)) +  # only odd years will show
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.5, 0.9)) +
  labs(
    title = "Digital Device Access by Disability Status",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Proportion with Internet Access",
    color = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title= element_text(size = 20),
    axis.title.x= element_text(size = 15),
    axis.title.y= element_text(size = 15)
  )


Grants Statistics Table
pre_years <- c("y2016", "y2017", "y2018", "y2019", "y2020", "y2021", "y2022")
post_years <- c("y2023", "y2024")

pre_stats <- df5 %>%
  select(all_of(pre_years)) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(unlist(.)),
    Median = median(unlist(.)),
    SD = sd(unlist(.)),
    Min = min(unlist(.)),
    Max = max(unlist(.))
  )

post_stats <- df5 %>%
  select(all_of(post_years)) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(unlist(.)),
    Median = median(unlist(.)),
    SD = sd(unlist(.)),
    Min = min(unlist(.)),
    Max = max(unlist(.))
  )

change_stats <- post_stats - pre_stats

# Add period labels
pre_stats$Period <- "Pre-Policy (2016–2022)"
post_stats$Period <- "Post-Policy (2023–2024)"
change_stats$Period <- "Change (Post - Pre)"

summary_stats <- bind_rows(pre_stats, post_stats, change_stats) %>%
  select(Period, Mean, Median, SD, Min, Max) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, 0))
## Warning: There was 1 warning in `mutate()`.
## ℹ In argument: `across(where(is.numeric), round, 0)`.
## Caused by warning:
## ! The `...` argument of `across()` is deprecated as of dplyr 1.1.0.
## Supply arguments directly to `.fns` through an anonymous function instead.
## 
##   # Previously
##   across(a:b, mean, na.rm = TRUE)
## 
##   # Now
##   across(a:b, \(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE))
apa_table <- flextable(summary_stats) %>%
  set_header_labels(
    Period = "Period",
    Mean = "M",
    Median = "Median",
    SD = "SD",
    Min = "Min",
    Max = "Max"
  ) %>%
  autofit() %>%
  theme_vanilla() %>%
  align(align = "center", part = "all")

apa_table
Period

M

Median

SD

Min

Max

Pre-Policy (2016–2022)

536,320

489,759

153,654

363,011

1,347,585

Post-Policy (2023–2024)

590,572

538,070

180,968

410,963

1,397,328

Change (Post - Pre)

54,252

48,311

27,313

47,952

49,743

Model 1: Multiple Regression
Running the models
df6$loans <- as.numeric(df6$loans)
df6$grant <- as.numeric(df6$grant)
df6 <- df6%>%
  mutate(grants_z = scale(grant)[, 1])

model1 <- lm(grants_z ~ policy + loans, data = df6)
model2 <- lm(loans ~ policy + grants_z, data = df6)

summary(model1)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = grants_z ~ policy + loans, data = df6)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -1.6835 -0.5051 -0.2129  0.1853  4.5982 
## 
## Coefficients:
##               Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept) -3.819e-01  5.768e-02  -6.621 1.01e-10 ***
## policy       2.884e-01  1.018e-01   2.833  0.00481 ** 
## loans        3.352e-04  3.482e-05   9.626  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.9054 on 455 degrees of freedom
##   (1 observation deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1854, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1818 
## F-statistic: 51.76 on 2 and 455 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
summary(model2)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = loans ~ policy + grants_z, data = df6)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -2481.3  -577.8  -227.1   321.4  7344.0 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)   956.10      59.01  16.203   <2e-16 ***
## policy        -29.74     126.00  -0.236    0.813    
## grants_z      504.72      52.43   9.626   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 1111 on 455 degrees of freedom
##   (1 observation deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.1711, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1674 
## F-statistic: 46.96 on 2 and 455 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
Table Format
modelsummary(
  list("Grants" = model1, "Loans" = model2),
  stars = TRUE,
  statistic = "({std.error})",
  fmt = 3,
  gof_map = data.frame(
    raw = c("r.squared", "adj.r.squared", "f.statistic", "nobs", "rmse"),
    clean = c("R²", "Adj. R²", "F-statistic", "N", "RMSE"),
    fmt = c(3, 3, 2, 0, 2)
  )
)
Grants	Loans
+ p < 0.1, * p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001
(Intercept)	-0.382***	956.101***
(0.058)	(59.009)
policy	0.288**	-29.743
(0.102)	(126.003)
loans	0.000***	
(0.000)	
grants_z		504.720***
(52.432)
R²	0.185	0.171
Adj. R²	0.182	0.167
N	458	458
RMSE	0.90	1107.34
Model 2: Two-Way Fixed Effects
Grants & Device Loans
Running the model
df6 <- df6%>%
  mutate(grants_z = scale(grant)[, 1])

model <- feols(loans ~ grants_z * policy | state + year, data = df6)
## NOTE: 1 observation removed because of NA values (LHS: 1).
## The variable 'policy' has been removed because of collinearity (see $collin.var).
summary(model)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: loans
## Observations: 458
## Fixed-effects: state: 51,  year: 9
## Standard-errors: Clustered (state) 
##                 Estimate Std. Error  t value Pr(>|t|)    
## grants_z        -887.206    557.442 -1.59157 0.117786    
## grants_z:policy  301.289    128.488  2.34488 0.023044 *  
## ... 1 variable was removed because of collinearity (policy)
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 427.1     Adj. R2: 0.85807 
##               Within R2: 0.055183
Table format
modelsummary(
  model,
  stars = TRUE,
  fmt = 3,                         # 3 decimal places
  statistic = "({std.error})",     # standard errors in parentheses
  output = "markdown"              # clean table in console/Viewer
)
(1)
+ p < 0.1, * p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001
grants_z	-887.206
(557.442)
grants_z × policy	301.289*
(128.488)
Num.Obs.	458
R2	0.877
R2 Adj.	0.858
R2 Within	0.055
R2 Within Adj.	0.050
AIC	6969.9
BIC	7221.7
RMSE	427.07
Std.Errors	by: state
FE: state	X
FE: year	X
Device Loans & Internet Access
Running the model
df7 <- df7%>%
  mutate(grants_z = scale(grant)[, 1])

model <- feols(internet ~ loans * policy | state + year, data = df7)
## The variable 'policy' has been removed because of collinearity (see $collin.var).
summary(model)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: internet
## Observations: 204
## Fixed-effects: state: 51,  year: 4
## Standard-errors: Clustered (state) 
##               Estimate Std. Error   t value Pr(>|t|) 
## loans        -3.01e-06   5.66e-06 -0.532369  0.59683 
## loans:policy  1.83e-06   3.61e-06  0.508103  0.61362 
## ... 1 variable was removed because of collinearity (policy)
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.036138     Adj. R2: 0.717256
##                  Within R2: 0.001375
Table Format
modelsummary(
  model,
  stars = TRUE,
  fmt = 3,                         # 3 decimal places
  statistic = "({std.error})",     # standard errors in parentheses
  output = "markdown"              # clean table in console/Viewer
)
(1)
+ p < 0.1, * p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001
loans	-0.000
(0.000)
loans × policy	0.000
(0.000)
Num.Obs.	204
R2	0.794
R2 Adj.	0.717
R2 Within	0.001
R2 Within Adj.	-0.012
AIC	-663.8
BIC	-478.0
RMSE	0.04
Std.Errors	by: state
FE: state	X
FE: year	X
Device Loans & Digital Device Access
Running the model
df7 <- df7%>%
  mutate(grants_z = scale(grant)[, 1])

model <- feols(device ~ loans * policy | state + year, data = df7)
## The variable 'policy' has been removed because of collinearity (see $collin.var).
summary(model)
## OLS estimation, Dep. Var.: device
## Observations: 204
## Fixed-effects: state: 51,  year: 4
## Standard-errors: Clustered (state) 
##                Estimate Std. Error   t value Pr(>|t|) 
## loans        -5.749e-06   4.75e-06 -1.209793  0.23205 
## loans:policy  8.210e-07   3.78e-06  0.217204  0.82893 
## ... 1 variable was removed because of collinearity (policy)
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## RMSE: 0.037154     Adj. R2: 0.651256
##                  Within R2: 0.00302
Table Format
modelsummary(
  model,
  stars = TRUE,
  fmt = 3,                         # 3 decimal places
  statistic = "({std.error})",     # standard errors in parentheses
  output = "markdown"              # clean table in console/Viewer
)
(1)
+ p < 0.1, * p < 0.05, ** p < 0.01, *** p < 0.001
loans	-0.000
(0.000)
loans × policy	0.000
(0.000)
Num.Obs.	204
R2	0.746
R2 Adj.	0.651
R2 Within	0.003
R2 Within Adj.	-0.010
AIC	-652.5
BIC	-466.7
RMSE	0.04
Std.Errors	by: state
FE: state	X
FE: year	X
Model 3: Regression Discontinuity
Run Model
model1 <- rdrobust(y = df6$loans, x = df6$grant, c = 610000)
summary(model1)
## Sharp RD estimates using local polynomial regression.
## 
## Number of Obs.                  458
## BW type                       mserd
## Kernel                   Triangular
## VCE method                       NN
## 
## Number of Obs.                  364           94
## Eff. Number of Obs.              96           38
## Order est. (p)                    1            1
## Order bias  (q)                   2            2
## BW est. (h)               90099.919    90099.919
## BW bias (b)              122855.462   122855.462
## rho (h/b)                     0.733        0.733
## Unique Obs.                     364           94
## 
## =============================================================================
##         Method     Coef. Std. Err.         z     P>|z|      [ 95% C.I. ]       
## =============================================================================
##   Conventional  1220.203   510.294     2.391     0.017   [220.045 , 2220.361]  
##         Robust         -         -     2.391     0.017   [253.154 , 2554.803]  
## =============================================================================
RD Plot
rdplot(y = df6$loans, x = df6$grant, c = 610000,
       p=1,
       title = "Regression Discontinuity Plot",
       x.label = "Grants",
       y.label = "Loans")


RD Cutoff vs Treatment Effect Plot
cutoffs <- seq(580000, 640000, by = 5000)

estimates <- numeric(length(cutoffs))

for (i in seq_along(cutoffs)) {
  result <- rdrobust(y = df6$loans, x = df6$grant, c = cutoffs[i], covs = df6$year)
  estimates[i] <- result$Estimate[1]
}

results_df6 <- data.frame(cutoff = cutoffs, estimate = estimates)

plot(results_df6$cutoff, results_df6$estimate, type = "b",
     main = "RD Treatment Effect by Cutoff",
     xlab = "Cutoff (c)", ylab = "Estimated Treatment Effect")
